papers.title: Nano-mechanical properties of Fe-Mn-Al-C lightweight steels
papers.paper_scope: Nano-mechanical properties of duplex Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels with and without 5 wt.% Ni as a function of annealing temperature (500–1050°C), focusing on the contributions of κ-carbides and B2 intermetallic phases through nano-indentation and ab-initio DFT calculations.

alloys.alloys_notes: Table 1 lists the carbon content as 0.5 wt.%, whereas the main text and Materials section specify 0.8 wt.%.
Table 1 lists the carbon content as 0.5 wt.%, whereas the main text and Materials section specify 0.8 wt.%.

processes.description: Induction furnace melting, solution heat treatment at 1250°C for 35 min in a protective argon atmosphere, water quenching, and subsequent annealing at 500–1050°C to engineer ordered precipitate morphology.

Induction furnace melting, solution heat treatment at 1250°C for 35 min in a protective argon atmosphere, water quenching, and subsequent annealing at 500–1050°C to engineer ordered precipitate morphology.

structures.overall_structure: Duplex-like matrix replaced by γ-phase containing cuboid κ-carbides and coarse B2 particles, with prior α-phase regions transformed to B2 stringer bands at 500°C.
γ-phase matrix with cuboid κ-carbides and coarse B2 particles, and B2 stringer bands replacing the prior α-phase at 700°C.
Duplex microstructure of γ and α at 900°C; γ contains coarse B2 particles and α contains nano-sized disc-like B2 precipitates.
Duplex microstructure of γ and α at 1050°C; γ contains coarse B2 particles and α contains nano-sized disc-like B2 precipitates.
Duplex microstructure of γ and α at 500°C; γ contains cuboid κ-carbides and coarse B2 particles, α contains needle-like κ-carbides.
Duplex microstructure of γ and α at 700°C; γ contains cuboid κ-carbides and coarse B2 particles, α contains needle-like κ-carbides.
Duplex microstructure of γ and α at 900°C; γ contains coarse B2 particles and α contains nano-sized disc-like B2 precipitates.
Duplex microstructure of γ and α at 1050°C; γ contains coarse B2 particles and α contains nano-sized disc-like B2 precipitates.

computational_details.simulation_method: Density Functional Theory (DFT) with generalized gradient approximation (GGA-PBE) in spin-polarized formalism
computational_details.potential_model: Ultrasoft pseudo-potentials based on modified RRKJ scheme
computational_details.validation_against_experiment: Calculated elastic properties of κ-carbides and B2 intermetallics compared with nano-indentation trends; used to rationalize hardness differences between NiAl-type and FeAl-type B2 and Mn-content dependence of κ-phase.

analytical_model.equation_role: Fitting simulated unit cell volumes to calculate bulk modulus
analytical_model.model_note: Various unit cell volumes were simulated and fitted to obtain bulk modulus.
Used to derive dynamical matrices from electronic subsystem linear response.
30×30×30 mesh used for electronic density of states.

unmapped_findings: Table 1 lists the nominal carbon content as 0.5 wt.% for both alloys, whereas the main text and Materials section consistently specify 0.8 wt.%.

Figure 4 and Table 2 contain quantitative nano-indentation hardness values and load-depth curves for various individual and composite phases, but the specific numerical data were not included in the provided text.

Nano-indentation measured compound hardness of the following combined phases because individual nano-sized phase properties could not be isolated: γ + cuboid κ, γ + cuboid κ + microsized B2, γ + microsized B2, α + needle-shape κ, and α + nano-disk B2.

DFT calculations determined the elastic constants and Young's modulus of nano-sized cuboid κ-phase as a function of Mn content, but specific numerical values for elastic constants or moduli were not provided in the text.

Qualitative hardness trends indicate that Ni-containing samples exhibit higher hardness due to NiAl-type B2 compared with FeAl-type B2 in Ni-free samples, because Ni-Al bonds are stronger than Fe-Al bonds.
At annealing temperatures of 900°C and above, nano-sized B2 discs in the ferrite phase yield higher hardness in Ni-containing samples than in Ni-free samples.
At 700°C and below, Ni-free samples show lowered hardness because micrometre-sized needles of κ-carbide replace the nano-sized B2 discs in ferrite.

In Ni-containing samples at 700°C and below, the prior ferrite phase is replaced by B2 stringer bands that have lower hardness than the Ni-Al nano-discs formed at higher temperatures, attributed to lower Ni content in the stringer bands.
The hardness of needle-like κ-carbides formed in the α phase was reported to be a function of Mn content, but quantitative hardness values were not provided in the text.
